what_it_does: Finds linear axes explaining the largest variance in the feature matrix.
best_use_case: First-pass QC, batch checks, plate effects, broad treatment separation and reportable sanity checks.
what_results_mean: Separation along PCs suggests large systematic differences, but the cause may be biology, batch, dose, focus or segmentation quality.
when_not_to_use: Do not use PCA alone to claim mechanism of action or class prediction.
main_caveats: Dominated by high-variance structure; nonlinear or subtle phenotypes may be missed.

what_it_does: Places profiles in a low-dimensional map that tries to preserve local neighbourhoods.
best_use_case: Exploring whether treatments form local groups after PCA/QC and comparing with nearest-neighbour tables.
what_results_mean: Nearby points may share morphology, but distances and cluster shapes are not globally quantitative.
when_not_to_use: Do not use UMAP alone for statistical claims or as the only evidence for clusters.
main_caveats: Sensitive to parameters, random seed, scaling and preprocessing; attractive plots can overstate structure.

what_it_does: Finds the closest profiles or compounds using a chosen distance metric.
best_use_case: Checking whether replicates find each other, identifying compounds with similar phenotypes, and selecting local contrasts for explanation.
what_results_mean: A close neighbour suggests phenotypic similarity under the chosen feature space and preprocessing.
when_not_to_use: Avoid interpreting nearest neighbours when replicate QC is poor or batch/plate explains major PCs.
main_caveats: Depends strongly on preprocessing, feature selection, metric and whether profiles or compound medians are used.

what_it_does: Aggregates profiles by compound and displays pairwise compound distances.
best_use_case: Showing relationships among treatments and controls in a compact report figure.
what_results_mean: Blocks of low distance can indicate groups of similar phenotypes.
when_not_to_use: Do not treat heatmap clusters as formal MOA classes without additional evidence.
main_caveats: Aggregation can hide replicate variability and dose/plate effects.

what_it_does: Reorders a distance heatmap by hierarchical clustering to show related groups.
best_use_case: Exploratory grouping of compounds and controls after QC has passed.
what_results_mean: Adjacent compounds in the dendrogram are relatively similar under the selected distance metric.
when_not_to_use: Do not over-interpret dendrogram branch lengths when the underlying silhouette/replicate metrics are weak.
main_caveats: Cluster order can change with metric, linkage, filtering and aggregation choices.

what_it_does: Partitions profiles into a chosen number of spherical-ish clusters.
best_use_case: Fast exploratory cluster labels when the approximate number of groups is being explored.
what_results_mean: Cluster membership indicates similar profiles under the selected feature space, not necessarily shared biology.
when_not_to_use: Poor choice when clusters are non-spherical, uneven in size, or when k is arbitrary and unsupported.
main_caveats: Requires k; sensitive to scaling and initialisation; should be checked with silhouette and replicates.

what_it_does: Builds nested clusters from pairwise distances.
best_use_case: Exploring hierarchical relationships among compounds or profile medians.
what_results_mean: Clusters summarise relative similarity at chosen cut levels.
when_not_to_use: Avoid using as a final classifier without stability and biological validation.
main_caveats: Sensitive to linkage and distance metric; can force structure into continuous gradients.

what_it_does: Finds dense regions and labels sparse points as noise.
best_use_case: Detecting strong dense phenotypic groups or outliers without specifying k.
what_results_mean: Noise labels can identify unusual or poorly grouped profiles.
when_not_to_use: Not ideal when densities vary greatly or the dataset is small/high-dimensional.
main_caveats: Sensitive to epsilon/min-samples and feature scaling.

what_it_does: Measures whether profiles from the same treatment are more similar to each other.
best_use_case: Deciding whether a phenotype is reproducible enough to interpret or model.
what_results_mean: Higher within-treatment correlation supports reproducibility; low values suggest weak, noisy or heterogeneous profiles.
when_not_to_use: Do not use ML/MOA/CLIPn claims if replicate reproducibility is poor and unresolved.
main_caveats: Large replicate counts can produce huge pairwise tables; summaries should be read first.

what_it_does: Quantifies how much metadata such as plate or batch explains leading PCs.
best_use_case: Checking whether preprocessing/normalisation reduced unwanted technical structure.
what_results_mean: High eta-squared for plate/batch suggests technical effects may be driving the feature space.
when_not_to_use: Single-group variables are not testable and should not be interpreted as evidence of no batch effect.
main_caveats: One-variable-at-a-time checks do not prove causal batch correction.

what_it_does: Compares query profiles with class or pseudo-anchor centroids.
best_use_case: Generating hypotheses when reference classes or pseudo-anchors are available and class sizes are adequate.
what_results_mean: High similarity to a class centroid suggests phenotypic resemblance to that class.
when_not_to_use: Do not use when classes have too few examples, poor replicate QC or strong confounding.
main_caveats: Centroids hide within-class heterogeneity; output is a hypothesis, not proof of mechanism.

what_it_does: Builds data-driven anchor groups and labels them optionally using phenotype annotations.
best_use_case: When true MOA labels are missing but the user wants structured phenotype hypotheses.
what_results_mean: A pseudo-anchor label summarises a group of similar profiles; phenotype labels should be reviewed via audit tables.
when_not_to_use: Do not use as a replacement for curated MOA labels or external validation.
main_caveats: Clusters can reflect batch, dose or dominant morphology rather than mechanism.

what_it_does: Learns nonlinear feature patterns that separate supplied classes.
best_use_case: Software smoke tests, labelled benchmark data, or explainability when labels are reliable and sample size is adequate.
what_results_mean: Good cross-validation suggests labels are learnable from morphology under the train/test design.
when_not_to_use: Avoid when labels are weak, leakage-prone, confounded or there are too few samples per class.
main_caveats: Feature importance can be biased; performance must be checked with held-out or grouped validation.

what_it_does: Learns a linear boundary between classes.
best_use_case: Simple interpretable baselines and labelled data with moderate sample size.
what_results_mean: Good performance means classes are linearly separable after preprocessing.
when_not_to_use: Not suitable for complex nonlinear phenotypes unless used as a baseline.
main_caveats: Coefficients depend on scaling and correlated features.

what_it_does: Measures how much model performance drops when a feature is shuffled.
best_use_case: Global feature ranking after a model has shown credible validation performance.
what_results_mean: Large drops suggest the model relies on that feature.
when_not_to_use: Do not use if the classifier itself has poor or leakage-prone performance.
main_caveats: Correlated features can share importance; not a causal explanation.

what_it_does: Estimates local/global contributions of features to a model prediction or contrast.
best_use_case: Explaining compound-vs-control or compound-vs-neighbour differences after QC and valid model fitting.
what_results_mean: High SHAP values indicate features the fitted model used for that contrast.
when_not_to_use: Do not treat SHAP as biological proof or use it when the contrast has too few profiles.
main_caveats: Model-dependent, sensitive to background selection and correlated features.

what_it_does: Attempts to learn a latent representation across datasets/references using the CLIPn backend.
best_use_case: Optional comparison layer when users want to benchmark against CLIPn or integrate multiple compatible datasets.
what_results_mean: Useful only if the backend genuinely ran and latent diagnostics support structure.
when_not_to_use: Do not prioritise CLIPn over classical/NN/QC routes when diagnostics are weak; single-dataset splits are software tests, not true integration benchmarks.
main_caveats: Backend/API/GPU fragile; published claims may not hold on every dataset; always report provenance and fallback status.

what_it_does: Runs pseudo-anchor/MOA analysis on CLIPn latent features when CLIPn succeeds.
best_use_case: Secondary analysis to ask whether latent-space groups agree with feature-space MOA or known biology.
what_results_mean: Agreement with classical MOA can strengthen confidence; disagreement should trigger review.
when_not_to_use: Do not run or interpret as CLIPn biology when PCA fallback was used.
main_caveats: Latent-space clustering can be hard to interpret and should be clearly labelled separately from feature-space MOA.
